UI_ETW_Options_ReadMe_tooltip: Please note that there are both sandbox settings and mod option settings in this mod. In 99% of the cases whatever you want to enable/disable/change can be changed. This includes sound notifications, hotkeys, this UI adjustments, and a hundred of other settings. Sandbox settings are in sandbox settings, obviously. Mod Options can be found Esc -> Options -> (Mods) -> Evolving Traits World. Hotkeys can be found in Keybinds in Esc -> Options -> Key Bindings.

UI_EatSmart_goalCalories1Enable_tooltip: Enables the option to eat just the amount of food to reach the 'Calories 1' goal if possible. <LINE> If it's not possible to reach the goal <LINE> (there isn´t enough food or the goal has been exceeded), the option will be disabled.
UI_EatSmart_goalCalories1Value_tooltip: Amount of calories that will be reached if possible. <LINE> If it's not possible to reach the goal <LINE> (there isn´t enough food or the goal has been exceeded), the option will be disabled.
UI_EatSmart_goalCaloriesMaxEnable_toolti: Enables the option to eat the necessary amount of food for the character to reach the maximum calories the character can have (3700 calories).
UI_EatSmart_goalCaloriesToGainWeightEnab: Goal Calorie Threshold To Gain Weight

Enable the option to eat the required amount of food for the character to reach the calorie threshold at which they begin to gain weight. This threshold depends on the character's weight and traits (slow or fast metabolism). They will consume 5 more calories than this limit, so you can see when the weight gain indicator disappears in the character's information tab.
UI_EatSmart_goalHunger0Enable_tooltip: Enables the option to eat just the amount of food to reach 0 % Hunger if possible. <LINE> If it's not possible to reach the goal <LINE> (there isn´t enough food or you have exceeded the goal), the option will be disabled.
UI_EatSmart_goalHunger1Enable_tooltip: Enables the option to eat just the amount of food to reach the given amount as 'Hunger 1' if possible. <LINE> If it's not possible to reach the goal <LINE> (there isn´t enough food or the goal has been exceeded), the option will be disabled. <LINE> 0 % means not hungry, 100 % means starvation. <LINE> Level 1 of the character's hunger state appears when 15% hunger.  <LINE> Level 2 when 25% hunger. <LINE> Level 3 when 45% hunger. <LINE> Level 4 when 70% hunger.
UI_EatSmart_goalHunger1Value_tooltip: Amount of hunger [%] that will be reached if possible.
UI_EatSmart_goalProteins1Enable_tooltip: Enables the option to eat just the amount of food to reach the 'Proteins 1' goal if possible. <LINE> If it's not possible to reach the goal <LINE> (there isn´t enough food or the goal has been exceeded), the option will be disabled. <LINE>There is a x1.5 multiplier for strenght XP gain when proteins are more than 50 and less than 300. <LINE>There is a x0.7 multiplier for strenght XP gain when proteins are less than -300.
UI_EatSmart_goalProteins1Value_tooltip: Amount of proteins that will be reached if possible. <LINE> If it's not possible to reach the goal <LINE> (there isn´t enough food or the goal has been exceeded), the option will be disabled. <LINE>There is a x1.5 multiplier for strenght XP gain when the character's proteins are more than 50 and less than 300. <LINE>There is a x0.7 multiplier for strenght XP gain when the character's proteins are less than -300.
UI_EatSmart_goalThirst0Enable_tooltip: Enables the option to eat just the amount of food to reach 0 % Thirst if possible. <LINE> If it's not possible to reach the goal <LINE> (there isn´t enough food or you have exceeded the goal), the option will be disabled.

UI_TWF_BF_SLIDER_DEPENDS_HINT: Sliders only work when feature above is enabled.
UI_TWF_BF_USE_TWF_AIMDIST_TT: Enable the Better Fishing override for cast/aim distance. When enabled, the Aim Distance slider below sets the maximum tiles.
UI_TWF_BF_USE_TWF_SHORE_TT: Enable a lighter, smaller-radius shore detection. When enabled, the Shore Radius slider below defines the search radius.
UI_TWF_BF_USE_TWF_SIZETABLES_TT: Switch to Better Fishing catch-chance tables (skill size limits and distribution). Requires reload of your savegame.

UI_VorpallySauced_CompIntro_I:
FOUND NEAR MULDRAUGH - [date smudged] 
 
I don't know who's gonna read this. Maybe nobody. But someone's gotta write it down before we all forget what's real and what ain't. 
 
It started maybe a week after the blockade. Tommy Culver - you know Tommy, works at the Zippee Mart - he'd been using his granddaddy's hatchet to put down the dead ones. Said he noticed it first around kill fifteen or so. The blade felt different. Lighter. Like it WANTED to swing. 
 
We thought he was losing it. Stress'll do that. But then Mary Chen said the same thing about her kitchen knife. And old Harlan with his splitting maul. 
 
The weapons are waking up. 
 
Pastor Mike says it's the souls of the dead, trapped in the metal. Revenge or something. The Wilkins boy - the one who watches all them horror tapes - he says it's like Evil Dead. The blood's doing something. 
 
All I know is this: you kill enough of them with the same tool, something STIRS inside it. We're calling it "The Stir" for now. First stage, the weapon gets a little something extra. Folks been calling these early changes "Marks." 
 
What follows is what we've documented so far. God help us all. 
 
- R.L. Morrison, Muldraugh Survivors Coalition
UI_VorpallySauced_CompIntro_II:
RECOVERED FROM WEST POINT SAFEHOUSE - FIELD NOTES, UNDATED 
 
Continued observations on the phenomenon locally designated "The Stir." 
 
Subject weapons that have accumulated significant termination counts (est. 15-30 confirmed kills) exhibit measurable changes that I cannot explain within any existing framework. 
 
I have performed Rockwell hardness testing on three subject blades. Civilian hatchet ("Tommy's hatchet" per local designation): initial reading 56 HRC, consistent with standard carbon steel. Current reading: 63 HRC. This is metallurgically impossible without rehardening. No heat source was applied. I tested twice. The numbers are correct. 
 
Edge retention on subject blades has increased approximately 340% over baseline. One machete has maintained its factory edge through an estimated 200+ impacts with bone and cartilage. I resharpened my control blade four times in the same period. 
 
Weight measurements are inconsistent. The same axe reads differently on the same scale taken minutes apart. Variance of +/- 0.3kg with no material added or removed. The old coal miner (civilian, name withheld) observed me struggling with this and said: "The hills have always known. You can't weigh something that's waking up." 
 
I am a scientist. I do not have a theory for this. I have measurements that should not exist. 
 
PRACTICAL OBSERVATION: Weapons exhibiting these enhanced properties demonstrate approximately 2-3x the performance gains of initial Marks. Survivors should be encouraged to maintain and develop their affected weapons rather than switching to fresh equipment. The bond appears cumulative and irreversible. 
 
- Dr. Sarah Chen, CDC Field Team 7 
- [Note in different handwriting: "Found Auntie Sarah's notes. Didn't find her. - Mary Chen"]
UI_VorpallySauced_CompIntro_III:
JOURNAL FRAGMENT - WATER DAMAGED - DATE UNKNOWN 
 
[Previous pages missing or illegible] 
 
...and that's when I knew it wasn't just in my head. 
 
I set the machete down on the kitchen counter last night. Blade pointing left, same as always. Woke up and it was turned around. Handle toward my hand. Like it was ready for me. 
 
Could've been the wind. Could've been anything. But the balance is different now too. It used to pull to the right - cheap blade, always did. Now it sits perfectly in my grip. I didn't adjust anything. It adjusted to ME. 
 
Old Harlan says his maul is the same way. Says the head feels heavier on the downswing, lighter on the return. "Like it knows which direction the dead ones are," he said. He won't look at it when he's not using it. 
 
The Wilkins boy calls these the "Violent" ones. Says the weapon's changing faster now. Adapting. He showed me his VHS of Hellraiser and said "that's what we're dealing with, man. Blood and pain and something that LIKES it." 
 
He might be crazy but he ain't wrong. 
 
The blade hasn't done anything I'd call scary. Not yet. But when I hold it, I can feel something in the metal. Something patient. Something that wasn't there before. 
 
If you're reading this and you got a weapon that's gone Violent - don't abandon it. Don't let it go. It chose you. For better or worse. 
 
[Remaining pages torn out]
UI_VorpallySauced_CompIntro_IV:
PRIVATE JOURNAL - PASTOR MICHAEL REEVES - ROSEWOOD 
 
The Lord tests us in ways I no longer understand. 
 
I blessed Brother Thompson's crowbar tonight. He asked me to. Said it felt wrong and he wanted God's hand on it. I held it and said the words and I felt... I don't know what I felt. Warmth. Not from the metal - from INSIDE the metal. Like holding a sleeping animal. 
 
I told him it was fine. I told him God's grace covered all things. 
 
I lied. 
 
Sister Martinez's fire axe burns cold to the touch now. COLD. And yet it cleaves through bone like butter left in the sun. She killed four of the risen dead in a single sweep yesterday. Four. With a fire axe. I counted the bodies. 
 
These weapons they call "Consuming" - they are no longer tools. When Thompson swings that crowbar, the air itself seems to scream. When Martinez holds her axe, her eyes go somewhere else. Somewhere I can't follow. 
 
The Sauced. The Vampiric. The Reaper's blade. 
 
These are not names we gave them. These are names they TOLD us. 
 
I do not know if God has armed us for the final battle or if we have merely traded our souls for survival. What I know is that when I held Thompson's crowbar and said my blessing, something in the metal heard me. 
 
And I am not certain it was God that answered. 
 
I have begun calling them Manifestations. The word feels right — it comes from the Latin, manifestare, to make visible. That is what these weapons are doing. Making something visible that was always there. Something in the metal, or in the blood, or in us. God forgive me, I do not know which. 
 
(Margin note, later date: "Tommy Culver's hatchet finally talked to him last night. He won't say what it said. He hasn't stopped crying.")
UI_VorpallySauced_CompIntro_V:
HANDWRITTEN ON MOTEL STATIONERY - DIXIE BOY MOTOR LODGE 
 
Okay so nobody believed me about this for like three weeks but I finally got proof. 
 
After a weapon gets its Mark - after the Stir takes hold - there's something ELSE that can happen. Something deeper. We're calling it "The Bond." 
 
I thought it was cool at first. Like some kind of superpower. Mary's kitchen knife started lasting longer than it had any right to. Harlan's maul hit harder on the downswing. Tommy's hatchet - the one that talked to him - it started finding the skull on its own. 
 
Then Rico tried to switch weapons. 
 
He'd been using a crowbar since week one. Good steel, heavy, reliable. It had Marks on it - we could all see the difference in how it moved. But he found a fire axe in the hardware store and figured bigger was better. Set the crowbar down on the kitchen table and walked out with the axe. 
 
He made it about forty feet. 
 
Said his hands started shaking first. Then his teeth. Then something in his chest went cold and tight, like a fist closing around his lungs. He turned around and the crowbar was where he'd left it. Sitting on the table. Pointing at the door. 
 
He picked it back up and everything stopped. 
 
Rico doesn't talk about it. But he sleeps with that crowbar now. Same as Tommy with the hatchet. Same as Martinez with the axe. They don't CHOOSE to carry them anymore. They just do. Like breathing. Like blinking. 
 
The Bonds are different from the Marks. Marks change how the weapon fights. Bonds change what the weapon IS. Some hit harder. Some swing faster. Some just... last longer than they have any right to. 
 
But here's what keeps me up at night: I don't think the Bonds are something we DO to the weapons. 
 
I think they're something the weapons do to US. 
 
UPDATE - three days later 
 
Rico tried to modify a SECOND crowbar. One that only had a Mark, not a Bond yet. He took nails and tried to spike it. Hammered them in, held it up, frowned. 
 
"It feels empty," he said. 
 
Same shape. Same weight, basically. But the Mark was just GONE. Like it had been living in the un-nailed iron and the nails kicked it out. 
 
We tested it with Mary's machete - she was further along, deep into a Bond. Tried to regrind a chip in the edge. Same result. Bond gone. The blade was the blade, but the THING inside it walked away. 
 
Then Tommy reground his Blooded hatchet for the hell of it. Took the edge way down, reshaped the whole bevel. The hatchet didn't seem to care. Whatever was in there had long since stopped caring about the steel. 
 
So I think there's a rule. Marks live in the metal. Bonds live in the metal. Blooded lives in YOU. 
 
- Jamie Wilkins, Age 17 
  Muldraugh Horror Club (membership: 1)
UI_VorpallySauced_CompIntro_VI:
FIELD REPORT - KNOX NATIONAL GUARD OUTPOST ECHO 
 
I'm not writing this for the scientists. The scientists are dead. 
 
I'm writing this because whoever finds this outpost needs to know what their weapons can do. The phenomenon the civilians call "The Stir" - it affects firearms too. If you've been using the same weapon since the outbreak, you already know. If you haven't, start now. 
 
What I've observed in my unit: 
 
Corporal Webb's M16 auto-corrects. Not mechanically - there's no computer in a '92 Colt. But it tracks center mass like it knows where the target is. Webb says he stopped aiming two weeks ago. He just points and the rifle does the rest. 
 
Sergeant Delgado's shotgun cycles faster than the action should physically allow. I've timed it. The mechanism moves quicker than the spring tension should permit. It shouldn't be possible. It is. 
 
My sidearm hasn't jammed in three hundred kills. It's a twenty-year-old Beretta that used to stovepipe every other magazine. Now it runs like it just left the factory. Better. Sometimes I think it fires before I pull the trigger. 
 
I don't care WHY this is happening. I care that it IS. 
 
STANDING ORDERS: Do NOT discard a weapon that's exhibiting. Maintain it. Use it. It will keep you alive longer than any replacement off the rack. What follows is a catalog of observed manifestations in affected firearms. 
 
- Gunnery Sergeant Marcus Cole 
  Kentucky National Guard (Dissolved)
UI_VorpallySauced_CompIntro_VII:
AUDIO TRANSCRIPT - RECOVERED TAPE RECORDER - WEST POINT 
 
[Recording begins mid-sentence] 
 
...don't even need to reload sometimes. I know that sounds insane. I KNOW. But I'm telling you, I've been carrying this Colt since the third day and something's changed. 
 
It holds more rounds now. I counted. Counted twice. Six rounds in a cylinder that only FITS six rounds. Except now it's seven. Then eight. Then ten. I stopped counting after that. 
 
[Sound of distant gunfire] 
 
The gun dreams. I know how that sounds but I'm not - I'm NOT crazy. When I sleep, I can feel it. Dreaming next to me. Hungry. Eager for morning. 
 
Terri's rifle is the same way. Reloads in half the time now. Not because she's faster - because the gun is. Like it's helping. Like it wants to get back in the fight. 
 
[Long pause] 
 
There's a guy came through from Fort Knox. Said before everything went dark, the eggheads were talking about "sympathetic metallurgy." Said the infection might have some kind of... I don't know... some kind of ability to imprint on inorganic matter. Make it part of the hive mind or whatever. 
 
I don't think that's it though. I think it's simpler. I think the guns are just learning. 
 
Learning to kill. 
 
And they're getting REALLY good at it. 
 
[Recording ends] 
 
NOTE: Speaker unidentified. Recorder found in abandoned vehicle on Route 31.
UI_VorpallySauced_CompIntro_VIII:
FINAL ENTRY - CHARRED PAGES - LOCATION UNKNOWN 
 
[Most of the journal is burned. Only fragments remain.] 
 
...finally understood what the old miner meant. "The hills have always known." 
 
My machete stopped wearing down around kill two hundred. By three hundred, the blade actually started to HEAL. I watched a chip fill itself in overnight. Metal doesn't do that. Metal CAN'T do that. 
 
But it did. 
 
And then it shed something. 
 
Small. Dark glass, almost black. Warm like skin. It fell from the blade while I was cleaning it — just dropped onto the floor and sat there. I picked it up and my hand started shaking. Not from fear. From FEELING. Like holding a small animal's heartbeat. 
 
I pressed it against Harlan's splitting maul, just to see. The Marks on the maul scrambled — shifted around, reformed into something new. The kills were still there. Everything it had learned, still there. But the pattern changed. 
 
I've started finding more of them. Always near the Blooded weapons. Always warm. 
 
...called it "becoming Blooded." Said his granddaddy's hunting rifle did the same thing back in '18. During the Spanish flu. Not the dead rising - just people dying. Millions of them. Said weapons that drink enough death eventually refuse to die themselves. That part keeps me up at night. It doesn't need to be THEM. It just needs to be death. 
 
I thought he was crazy. Now I understand. 
 
The weapon doesn't just bond to you at that point. It becomes you. Your heartbeat syncs with its weight. Your dreams are its dreams. When you swing it, you don't think about swinging anymore than you think about breathing. 
 
[Burn marks obscure several lines] 
 
...Tommy Culver finally stopped crying. His hatchet talked to him, he said. Told him its name. He won't share what it said, but he's different now. Calmer. Steadier. He holds the hatchet everywhere he goes now. Sleeps with it. 
 
The other day someone asked if they could borrow it. Tommy just smiled and said no. Not angry. Not scared. Just... sure. 
 
That scares me more than anything. 
 
[Final readable section] 
 
If you're reading this and you've got a weapon that's gone Blooded - congratulations. You've done something impossible. You've killed enough of them that the boundary between you and your weapon has dissolved. 
 
You're not a survivor anymore. 
 
You're something else entirely. 
 
[Fragment recovered from a separate page, same hand] 
 
A man came through last week with a Bonded crowbar. Asked if he could melt it down and recast it as a tool. I told him no - the Bond would unmake. He called me superstitious. Went off to do it anyway. 
 
Came back two days later. Quiet. Said the new tool felt like a stranger. 
 
But the woman with the Blooded axe has reforged a new haft three times in a month. The axe doesn't blink. The Bond doesn't live in the steel by then. It lives somewhere between her hands and the count of every skull she's split, and the steel is just where it shows up for work. 
 
That's the dividing line. Marks and Bonds need a vessel to be real. Blooded outgrows the vessel. You can give it new wood, new metal, new shape. The Stir leaps the gap. 
 
Don't reforge a weapon unless it's Blooded. Or unless you're ready to start over. 
 
- R.L. Morrison, Muldraugh 
  [Date burned away]

UI_VorpallySauced_JournalStatHint: Study the corresponding Compendium volume to reveal full details.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Bonding_ofBloodth: There's a hunger to it now. I can feel it when I swing. The damage is real but the want is realer.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Bonding_ofRebirth: It shouldn't still be in one piece. It is. I've stopped questioning it and started being grateful.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Bonding_ofTransce: Every axis improved. Not a lot on each one. But all of them. All at once. Weapon's become something it wasn't. I have too.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Bonding_ofWhisper: It takes more of them in one swing than it has any right to. I heard something in my head the first time it happened. Not words. Just a feeling. Satisfied.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Bonding_oftheCons: When it crits it really crits. And it crits a lot. I don't know what it consumed to get here. I don't think I want to know.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Bonding_oftheCove: It hits harder and it finds the vital spot more often. Like we made an agreement. Like something was agreed to that I wasn't part of, and I've benefited anyway.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Bonding_oftheUndy: The condition doesn't move. The max doesn't lower. It gets hit and it holds. I don't know what it learned from the blood, but it learned something.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Bonding_oftheWiel: It moves with me now. Fast and mean and exactly where I need it. The pushback is wrong — too strong. But the speed is wronger.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FBonding_ofTrueAi: It aims itself. That's not an exaggeration. The barrel finds the line before I've finished the thought. And then it fires faster than I can plan.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FBonding_oftheArs: More in the mag than there used to be and empty again faster than seems possible. Speed and volume together. It never runs dry at the wrong moment.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FBonding_oftheGun: Fifty percent faster reload and fires so fast between shots I stopped thinking of them as separate. This gun belongs to a different kind of fight.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FBonding_oftheMar: On target before I'm ready. Critting more than I should. This gun has a sense of occasion.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FBonding_oftheOpe: Hits harder, aims truer, gets on target faster. It's committed to the job in a way that goes beyond the metal it's made from.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FBonding_oftheVet: Accurate. Reliable. Durable. Reloads fast. It's like it's been through all of this before and decided to get better at it.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Annihilat: I don't aim for center mass anymore. The shot handles that part.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Brutal: Bodies are stopping faster. I don't know what that means for the gun. I know what it means for me.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Bullseye: I aimed it once today. Once. The rest was just reflex. All of them landed.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Combat: The delay between shots is almost nothing. The gun is angrier than I am.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Cyclic: It's barely pausing between rounds. My finger feels like it's not even part of the process.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Deadeye: Long shots. More crits. I'm watching them from farther away and putting them down cleaner. The gun made a deal with physics and didn't tell me.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Deadly: Running shot, panicking, low light — still connected. Something in the gun wants to connect.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Devastati: One burst today. One. I had to check that they were really down.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Execution: I stopped compensating for drift. The gun compensates.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Extended: I don't know how far that last shot went. Far enough. That's what matters.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_FullAuto: I lost track of the individual shots. There was just a sound and then silence.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Gunfighte: By the time they see me, I've already shot twice. The draw, the aim, the crit — it's all collapsed into one motion.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Honed: Something changed in the barrel, maybe. Or in me. The hits are heavier now.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Keen: The timing is better. The ones I shouldn't hit, I sometimes hit. More often now.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Long: Connected at a range that surprised me. Surprised them too, probably.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Marksmans: Every shot counts for more now. The accuracy, the crits, the time it takes to get there — all of it moving in the right direction.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Merciless: It puts the round where it needs to go. Every time. I'm mostly just holding it.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Operators: Accurate, fast, and it hurts when it hits. I don't know what I did to earn this.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Precise: Shots are landing where I point it. Actually landing. That wasn't always the case.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_QuickFire: The next shot is ready a little sooner than I expect. Small thing. Adds up.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Rapid: I'm shooting faster than I'm aiming. It doesn't seem to matter.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Reaching: I held the corner and still put shots downrange. More downrange than I should have.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Responsiv: It cycles back like it's in a hurry. I can keep the pace now.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Sauced: Critical hits I shouldn't have made, accuracy I didn't earn, damage that ends arguments. It's too much in all the right ways.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Savage: It's putting things down in fewer rounds. I'd call that a gift.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Sharp: Every shot punches deeper than it should. I checked the ammo. Same ammo.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Sighted: Whatever I point it at, it finds. Not every time. But closer to every time than before.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Snipers: The shot goes where my eyes say to send it. Not approximately. Exactly.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Steady: The barrel settles faster. The wobble is almost gone.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Tactical: Faster and more likely to connect. The rate of fire and the accuracy together — the gun is doing math I can't do.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_TrueAim: Stopped missing the second shot. That used to always drift.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Vicious: It found the gap twice today. The angle I needed and couldn't have made on my own.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Warzone: High damage and I can shoot fast and I can hit things far away. This is not a fair fight. I am grateful for that.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FPrefix_Zeroed: It's locked. I don't know how to describe it better than that. Locked on.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofAbundan: I shoot more and reload less. The math of it doesn't add up. I've checked.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofCapacit: A few more rounds before I'm fishing for a mag. Enough to make a difference.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofDestruc: I've stopped thinking of it as a per-shot calculation. It's more of a verdict.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofEnduran: This thing was not built to stop working. I've tested that.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofEternit: I don't know what to clean anymore. The wear just doesn't come.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofExecuti: I had a shot today I'll remember for a while. I won't describe it. I'll just remember it.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofFocus: The sight picture settles just a little faster. Small window. Worth having.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofFortitu: Three months. No jams from condition. Barely any wear I can see.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofHarm: Rounds are hitting a little harder. Same ammo. The gun does something to them.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofInfinit: I kept shooting and shooting and the mag kept going. I was almost embarrassed when it finally ran dry.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofInstinc: I'm on target before my brain finishes the command. The gap is closing.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofLethali: When it crits, the conversation ends. Immediately.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofLoading: I'm back in the fight before I expect to be. Good surprise.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofMastery: I cleaned it today out of habit. It didn't need it.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofPermane: I've stopped worrying about it breaking down. That used to take up a lot of space in my head.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofPrecisi: The crits sting more than they should. Something in the timing amplifies it.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofPreserv: Takes longer to wear out. Not forever. Just longer. That's enough.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofQuality: The degradation is slower. I'd say half as fast. Maybe better.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofQuietin: The report was softer. Not silent, but the block didn't turn. Fewer came.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofReliabi: Jammed once in the last month. Once. I almost didn't believe it when it happened.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofReloadi: Slightly faster getting the mag in. I feel it in the rhythm.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofResilie: Took it through conditions I wouldn't take my boots through. Still running.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofSilence: I fired from the roof. The horde below kept walking. Like I wasn't there.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofSpeed: The reload is almost automatic now. I don't think about it. I just do it and I'm done.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofStorage: Ran through a horde without reloading. Didn't used to be able to do that.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofSuppres: The shot left my hands and the sound stayed behind. The dead don't know I'm here.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofSwiftne: Hands do the job before I've finished the thought. I've had it for weeks and I'm still surprised.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofTrueFun: It feeds. Every time. I've stopped flinching when I pull the trigger.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_ofWoundin: They go down in fewer shots. That's the whole thing. Fewer shots.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_oftheArmo: More capacity, faster reload, hits harder. It's not one improvement. It's all of them, pointing in the same direction.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_oftheArse: I've started treating it like it's inexhaustible. That's an attitude you earn, not assume.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_oftheAssa: Aim fast, crit hard, hit heavy. Everything collapses into a single terrible moment.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_oftheComm: Reloads fast. Holds more. Doesn't wear down like it should. It's like it's trying to keep me alive.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_oftheFait: I put a round in the chamber and I know it will fire. That's not a small thing. That's not a small thing at all.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_oftheGuns: I reloaded mid-horde and nobody got me. That's not nothing.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_oftheImmo: It refuses to fail. Stubbornly, consistently, in defiance of everything. Refuses.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_oftheMaga: The mag holds more than the numbers on the side say. I counted. It holds more.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_oftheProf: It stays in good shape, aims quicker, and degrades like it's refusing to. This gun has professional standards.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_oftheQuic: Empty to loaded to shooting so fast I thought I'd miscounted the mag. I hadn't. It was just that quick.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_oftheRefl: I snap it up and it's already where I need it. Muscle memory except I didn't build this memory.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_oftheSauc: Durable and then more durable. I've stopped being surprised when it keeps working.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_oftheSold: Reload time almost nothing. Condition barely budges. Extra rounds in the mag. This is a weapon that was made for exactly this.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_FSuffix_oftheVete: Aims fast, reloads fast, and when it crits it really means it. Competence in one package.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Annihilati: I stopped counting what comes back out when I pull it away. Easier that way.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Apocalypti: It doesn't stop at one. The damage, the knockback, the spread — everything is too much, all at once.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Berserkers: Swinging it feels like falling forward. The momentum does the work. I'm just holding on.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Blinding: I can't see the individual swings anymore. Just the bodies going down.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Breaching: Door came off faster than it should have. I'll keep that in mind.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Brutal: Something's different about what it leaves behind. I try not to look.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Buckling: Hit one and it staggered sideways. Legs first. Like the ground gave out under it.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Cataclysmi: Hit one and knocked over two more. I'm not exaggerating. Two more.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Cleaving: I stopped tracking single targets. The arc just takes what's in front of it.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Crushing: The impact travels now. You feel it in your chest when it lands.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Deadly: It commits to the angle before I do. Crits more than they should.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Demolishin: Took out a door, got the frame, put a dent in the tree outside. This thing has opinions about obstacles.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Devastatin: It's not a fight anymore. It's arithmetic.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Earthshaki: They fall before I finish the swing. The air moves wrong and they go down.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Executione: It wants the skull. Every swing wants the skull. I've stopped arguing with it.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Extended: The nearest one is never where my eyes said it was. I keep hitting them anyway.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Felling: They're going down and staying down for a moment. Long enough to breathe. Long enough to matter.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Flooring: It dropped them. Not pushed back, not knocked sideways — straight down. Every time.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Forceful: They're stumbling back farther than they should be. Weight feels the same.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Honed: The resistance is gone. It goes through like they're not even trying to stop it.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Immolating: The last one caught fire. The one before it too. I don't think it's me doing that.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Inexhausti: I swung until I wanted to stop, not until I had to. That's new.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Keen: Something changed. I'm landing hits I shouldn't be, on the first try.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Lightning: My arm can barely keep up with it. My shoulder aches in a new way.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Long: Caught one at a distance that felt too far. Didn't miss.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Merciless: It doesn't miss the killing blow. I'm not sure it's me doing it anymore.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Phantom: It's everywhere before I've moved. The range and speed together make it feel like cheating.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Precise: Stopped hesitating after it. The swing finds the spot I mean it to.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Quick: The return is automatic now. I'm not controlling it, just riding it.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Rapid: Three of them in the time it used to take for two. I counted.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Reaching: I'm keeping more space than I used to and still making contact. Somehow.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Reapers: When it crits, it doesn't just kill. It ends. Something about the follow-through is final in a way I can't describe.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Relentless: Three hordes in an afternoon and I didn't have to stop once. The stamina cost just... isn't there.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Sauced: It's faster and it hits harder and it knows exactly where to land. All three, together, at once. I don't deserve this.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Savage: One of them didn't stand back up. Usually they keep trying. This one didn't.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Scorching: It left a mark on the floor where I set it down. A black ring. The wood underneath was fine.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Sharp: It cut deeper. I don't know how. It just did.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Shoving: I hit one through a doorframe today. It went back through the wall. I don't understand the physics.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Smoldering: The edge came back warm. Not hot — warm. Like it had been sitting in the sun.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Staggering: Hordes aren't crowding me the same. They keep bouncing off each other.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Sweeping: Two in one swing. I didn't mean to. But it happened again the next time.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Swift: It comes back around faster. Not a lot, but enough to feel.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Tireless: My arm should be burning. It's not. The weapon does something about that.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Titans: Every swing is an event. The damage, the pushback, whatever that did to the door. One hit. One event.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Vampiric: The crits feel wrong. Too clean. Too certain. Like it already knows which hits will count.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Vicious: I don't know what I'm doing different. The openings just appear.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Prefix_Whirlwind: I cleared the room before I realized I'd started. There was screaming and then there wasn't.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofCarnage: The arc keeps taking what's in front of it. I've mostly stopped trying to aim.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofCelerity: The swing comes back and I'm already swinging again. My arms figured it out before my head did.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofCollapse: They don't stay upright when I want them to go down. That's new.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofDestruct: I stopped counting hits altogether. The math got too easy.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofDistance: I stood back and they still couldn't close. They tried. It was almost sad.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofEnduranc: It's been through more than it shows. More than it should be able to handle.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofEntry: Took a door in two hits. Felt wrong how easy that was.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofEternity: It should be dust by now. The math doesn't work.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofExecutio: I had a crit today that made me take a step back and look at the weapon. Just look at it.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofForce: One hit and they're off balance. Two hits and they've stopped being my problem.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofFortitud: Used it hard for a week. Still looks like I found it yesterday.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofHarm: It hits a little harder. Not dramatically. But a little. And a little adds up.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofHaste: Slightly quicker than before. Just enough to change the rhythm.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofHushing: The shot was quieter. Not silent, but the echo died fast. The dead didn't come.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofImpact: They bounce back now. Just a little. Enough breathing room.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofIronWill: I kept swinging past the point I should have had to stop. The weapon carries some of the weight. I swear it does.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofLethalit: The crits aren't just bigger. They're final. One and done, when they land.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofMastery: It barely degrades. The edge holds. The metal holds. Everything holds.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofPermanen: I've stopped carrying a backup. I didn't decide to. I just stopped.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofPrecisio: When it crits, it really crits. The difference is significant. I felt it immediately.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofPreserva: The wear is slower. Not much, but I notice. It comes back from fights looking better than it should.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofQuality: I sharpened it twice in the last month. Used to be every few days.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofReach: Just a little more room. Just a little. That's enough.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofRending: It found a way to catch two at once. Same swing. I didn't plan it.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofResilien: I keep waiting for it to break. It keeps not breaking.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofSiege: I've stopped thinking of doors as obstacles.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofSilence: I fired from the second floor. Nothing turned. Nothing looked up. Like I wasn't there.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofVigor: My lungs feel wrong when I stop. Like I expected to be more tired than I am.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofWounding: They go down in fewer hits. I've been counting. Fewer every time.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_oftheApoca: More damage. More range. More of everything. Too much, probably. I don't care.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_oftheDrop: Knocked one to its knees. Ground cleared faster after that.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_oftheEarth: I hit one and it knocked the next one sideways. Couldn't have planned it.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_oftheEtern: It's built to last and it bites harder on the way out. I don't understand how those two things go together, but here we are.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_oftheFell: It drops them. Hard. I stopped watching once I knew they weren't getting up.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_oftheGhost: Fast and slippery and finding the gaps I didn't know were there. It feels like fighting from somewhere they can't quite see.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_oftheImmor: I think it's going to outlast me. I think it knows that too.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_oftheJugge: It hits, it pushes, it doesn't break. That's the whole thing. That's enough.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofthePhant: Three shots. Three down. The horde outside didn't flinch. The gun swallowed its own sound.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_ofthePolea: I'm hitting them before they expect to be hit. The range is wrong in a good way.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_oftheSauce: Durable and getting more durable. It's like it gets stubborn about falling apart.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_oftheSlaye: The crits are something else now. Critical chance, critical damage, regular damage — it all adds up into something I don't have words for.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_oftheTirel: I went longer than I've gone in months. Wasn't out of breath. Still don't understand it.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_oftheVoid: The reach, the speed, the spread — it covers more ground than any one weapon has the right to.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_Suffix_oftheWind: I stopped counting the time between hits. It got embarrassing how fast it was.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_oftheConsumed: It took something from them. I saw the edge change.
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_oftheCovenant: Every wound it deals, it keeps
UI_VorpallySauced_Lore_oftheWielder: It stopped being a separate thing. It's just my arm now.
UI_VorpallySauced_MagazineClamp: The magazine won't take more. Something about the fit changed.
UI_VorpallySauced_MagazineWarning: This weapon has a loaded magazine. Its capacity might change with the new marks. Continue?
UI_VorpallySauced_MoodNotify_Agitated2: An uneasy tremor in the grip.
UI_VorpallySauced_MoodNotify_Calm2: A quiet settles over the weapon.
UI_VorpallySauced_MoodNotify_Eager1: The grip tightens on its own.
UI_VorpallySauced_MoodNotify_Focused2: It narrows. Everything else falls away.
UI_VorpallySauced_MoodNotify_Focused3: It finds its line. Nothing else.
UI_VorpallySauced_NotifyShardGranted: Something fell from the weapon. Sharp. Warm.

VorpallySauced: Enhanced Tooltips Available 

Install 'TooltipLib' from the Steam Workshop for detailed weapon progression tooltips with stat breakdowns, kill progress, and tier colors.

Your weapons work normally without it, but legacy tooltips will be removed in a future update.

UI_VorpallySauced_Opt_PickerHint: Uncheck any affix to keep it from taking hold. Single-player only.
UI_VorpallySauced_Provenance_Bonded: The bond runs deeper than the form. It will survive the reforge.

UI_VorpallySauced_ReforgeBlocked: Only Blooded weapons can be reforged.
UI_VorpallySauced_ReforgeLoss_Bond: A deeper thing, unwoven. It was not yet ready to leap forms.
UI_VorpallySauced_ReforgeLoss_Mark: The form changed. The memory scattered.
UI_VorpallySauced_RerollComplete: The marks shifted. Something new took hold.
UI_VorpallySauced_TraitEffect_NocturnalN: Panic and stress fade at night. Night vision.
UI_VorpallySauced_TraitEffect_PanicNight: Panic and stress fade at night
UI_VorpallySauced_TraitNotify_SomethingC: It changed. Something beneath it shifted.
UI_VorpallySauced_TraitNotify_WeightShif: The weight shifts in your hand.

Reshapes a weapon's marks and bonds. Kills stay.
Uses: %1
UI_VorpallySauced_WeaponAwakened: It stirred. Something behind the metal woke up.
UI_VorpallySauced_WeaponBroken_TooltipLi: Broken. The Stir has gone quiet.
UI_VorpallySauced_WeaponMastery: It changed again. Deeper this time.

UI_trait_FastReaderDesc:

UI_trait_HemophobicDesc2:
Panic when performing first aid on self.

UI_trait_HerbalistDesc:

Needs more water to survive.

Experienced in hiking long distances.

You have trouble throwing anything away and tend to carry a lot of stuff with you.
You can carry additional load per each point of Strength
UI_trait_HomeCookDesc: You enjoy making your own meals and bake the occasional cake
UI_trait_HomichlophileDesc: You like the fog, something about it makes you feel cosy. You get calmer while you're outside in foggy weather. You slowly lose stress and panic depending on fog intensity.

UI_trait_Hunter2Desc: Has skills in Aiming, Short Blade, Sneaking, Trapping and Tracking.
UI_trait_InconspicuousDesc:
Less likely to be spotted by zombies.
(-50% chance of being spotted by a zombie.)
UI_trait_InsomniacDesc:
Slow loss of tiredness while sleeping.

UI_trait_IronGutDesc:
Less chance to have food illness.
(-50% chance of food illness. Food illness lasts ~ 55% shorter on average.
Raw eggs never cause food illness.)
UI_trait_KenshiDesc: Ki-ken-tai-ichi. I will pursue the unity of Mind, Body, and Blade
UI_trait_KnifeFighterDesc: You have practiced how to fight with a knife
UI_trait_LessSleepDesc:
Needs less sleep.
(-30% tiredness rate.
+25% tiredness recovery rate when sleeping.)
UI_trait_LightStepDesc: You are naturally light on your feet and make little sound moving
UI_trait_LowProfileDesc: You are a shady character and good at hiding
UI_trait_LowThirstDesc:
Needs to drink water less regularly.

If MDMA taken every 5 days, you can lose trait with no tier 2 withdrawals..

-Hunger, +Thirst, +Fatigue, -Endurance, +Stress, +Head Pain.

UI_trait_MechanicsDesc: Knows how to service and repair standard and heavy vehicles.

Has a Meth addiction.

Will lose trait if no Meth is done for 18-20 days.

Has an Opioid Dependence.

Decreases combat XP gains.

Drugs give a panic attack for entire duration of effect.

You have always loved pets more than people. Petting animals gives you mood boost: reduces panic, boredom, unhappiness and stress.
By deafult, can only occur once every 5 min and gives 5% mood boost. Adjustable in sandbox.

UI_trait_PolearmFighterDesc: You've mastered the art of fighting with long, pole-like weapons. From pitchforks to spears, you know how to keep threats at bay with reach and precision
UI_trait_PotHeaddesc:
Loves the Weeeeed, but lower Fitness levels.
Will lose trait if no Weed is done for 18-20 days.

UI_trait_SOHikerDesc:
Experienced in hiking long distances.
Knows some trapping recipes.
(Slightly reduces accumulation of hunger, thirst and fatigue if walking for a while without stopping.)
UI_trait_SOSmokerDesc:
Has a tobacco addiction.
Smoking tobacco reduces stress, unhappiness, and hunger.
Abstaining from smoking increases stress, unhappiness, and hunger.
May cough, attracting unwanted attention.
Slightly increases endurance loss while running or fighting.
UI_trait_Scout2Desc: Has skills in First Aid, Fishing and Foraging.
UI_trait_SlowHealerDesc:
Recovers slowly from injuries and illness.
(+20% severity of injuries. Does not apply to exercise fatigue.)
UI_trait_SlowLearnerDesc:
Decreased XP gains.
(-30% for all skills, except Fitness and Strength.)
UI_trait_SlowReaderDesc:
Takes longer to read books.
(-30% reading speed.)
UI_trait_SmokerDesc2:
Has a tobacco addiction.
Smoking tobacco reduces stress, unhappiness, and hunger.
Abstaining from smoking increases stress, unhappiness, and hunger.
May cough, attracting unwanted attention.
Slightly increases endurance loss while running or fighting.
UI_trait_SojutsuDesc: You practice Sojutsu, the Art of the Spear
UI_trait_SpeedDemonDesc:
The fast driver.
(+100% gear switching speed,
+15% top speed for all vehicles,
+200% engine loudness when reversing.)
UI_trait_StickFighterDesc: You have practiced stick fighting martial arts
UI_trait_SundayDriverDesc:
The very slow driver.
(-40% acceleration, -30% reverse acceleration, max speed of 30 MPH,
No change to engine loudness.)
UI_trait_ThinSkinnedDesc: Increased chance of scratches, lacerations, or bites breaking the skin.
UI_trait_WeakStomachDesc:
Higher chance to have food illness.(+100% chance)
(Food illness lasts shorter instead of longer (bug).)
UI_trait_accmetabolismdesc:
Trains Strength and Fitness faster if not hungry.
Prone to maintain ideal weight.
(+30% Strength and Fitness XP if hunger level not higher than Peckish,
-30% calorie burn if weight is less than 76,
+30% calorie burn if weight is over than 84.)
UI_trait_actionherodesc:
You live for the thick of the fight.
You deal only half as much normal damage, but you deal extra damage and have more critical chance the more enemies there are around you.
Your damage and critical chance scale based on number and distance of zombies around you.
UI_trait_advancedforagingdesc: Significantly increases the chances of finding specific items in Search Mode depending on your occupation.
UI_trait_albinodesc:
You've always had extreme sunlight sensitivity.
While outside and in daylight, you experience progressive head pain.
Hold an umbrella to reduce pain.
UI_trait_alcoholicdesc:
Has an alcohol addiction.
Abstaining from alcohol increases boredom, unhappiness, and thirst.
Prolonged abstinence increases fatigue, stress, headache, and nausea.
Alcohol's effect on drowsiness is reduced.
UI_trait_allergicdesc:
Sneezes involuntarily, attracting unwanted attention.
(Sneezes can be muffled with a tissue, handkerchief, or toilet paper.)
UI_trait_amputee_missingarm: I'm missing the arm for that...
UI_trait_amputeedesc:
One of your arms was amputated a long time ago.
You can not equip anything to your secondary, nor utilize your secondary arm for anything.
Your left arm can not be injured, and wounds that would otherwise be placed there are simply ignored.
UI_trait_anemicdesc: You bleed much more rapidly from wounds.
UI_trait_antigundesc:
You protest against guns and have no experience with them, making you worse at using them than others.
You take longer to aim, can not shoot far and become sad while using guns.
You receive 25% less aiming XP.
UI_trait_antiquedesc:
You know how to recognize an antique item when you see it.
You have a rare chance to find unique variants of items while searching industrial containers.
+1 Scavenging if Scavenging Skill mod enabled
UI_trait_asceticdesc:
You prefer to abstain from the unnecessary pleasures of the body and find spiritual fulfillment in simple things.
You dislike cooked food.
Pre-Packaged foods give no mood boost.
Uncooked Meals are neutral.
Very high chance to burn meals.
UI_trait_athleticdesc:
Faster running speed.
Can run for longer without tiring.
(-60% endurance loss when running or sprinting.
Can be gained by training Fitness to level 9.
Lost if Fitness is below 9.)

Better at chopping trees.
Faster axe swing.
(25% faster against trees, 25% faster against zombies.)
(+50% axe tree damage)

You never took much care of your dental health.
When consuming food, you may experience pain. Larger portions cause more pain.

You know how to force your way through crowds like a professional football player.
While Sprinting (not running), you knockdown zombies in your path.
Every zombie you knockdown depletes some endurance depending upon your Fitness level, so be careful charging into large crowds, as this may leave you exhausted.
Note: This trait overrides the Spear's charge ability.
UI_trait_bladetoolsdesc: Gains +20% XP when using Axes, Short Blades, or Long Blades with the main hand.
UI_trait_bladetwirldesc: You never thought all that swordplay would have practical use.
UI_trait_blissfuldesc:
You have a positive outlook at all times.
You regain happiness at an increased rate.
Boredom is reduced at an increased rate.
UI_trait_blunttwirldesc: Sure, you've capped a few knees in your time. That experience could prove useful.
UI_trait_bouncerdesc:
You've roughed people up now and again.
Whenever you are surrounded by two or more zombies,
you will automatically attempt to push them away.
UI_trait_bravedesc:
Less prone to becoming panicked.
(-70% panic, except for night terrors and phobias.)
UI_trait_breakintechniquedesc: Axes deals more damage to doors and barricades.
UI_trait_breathingtechdesc:
Increases endurance recovery while not moving.
(More effective while sitting.)
UI_trait_breathingtechniquedesc:
Increases endurance recovery while not moving.
(More effective while sitting.)
UI_trait_brokedesc:
Ouch! You start with a broken leg, a severely debilitating injury.
Note: Your leg will be splinted.

You had a traumatic experience with fire that affected you both physically and mentally.
You start out covered in bandaged burns on every part of your body.
Observing fire gives you panic and anxiety, this effect scales based on the distance between you and the fire.
Prevents you from throwing fire bombs, molotov cocktails and starting any kind of fire.

You may sometimes drop something you are holding.
Chance is based on carry weight and other traits such as luck or dextrous.
UI_trait_calmmindeddesc:
Remains calm in stressful situations.
(Maximum stress cannot exceed 50%,
Stress and panic decrease faster.)
UI_trait_claustrodesc:
Gets panicked when indoors.
(But not in 70-tiles + sized rooms. The smaller the room, the faster the panic increase.)
UI_trait_clumsydesc:
Makes more noise when moving.
(+20% footstep radius sound,
+10% chance to trip when run/sprint vaulting a low fence or getting attacked by lunging zombies.
Increased chance of falling when bumping into a zombie or survivor.)
UI_trait_commdriverdesc:
Reduces fatigue gain when driving a moving vehicle.
Able to repair heavy vehicles.
UI_trait_commercialdriverdesc:
Reduces fatigue gain when driving a moving vehicle.
Able to repair heavy vehicles.
UI_trait_cowardlydesc:
Especially prone to becoming panicked.
(+100% panic, except for night terrors and phobias.)
UI_trait_crueltydesc:
Increases combat XP gains.
(+25% XP for all weapon skills, Maintenance skill and Aiming skill.)
UI_trait_cuttingtoolsdesc: Gains +20% XP when using Axes, Short Blades, or Long Blades with the main hand.
UI_trait_deafdesc:
Very small perception radius and hearing range.
(Can't hear sound. Radio chatter doesn't appear.
Still able to watch TV due to closed captions.)

UI_trait_depressivedesc:
Often sad. May become depressed for no reason.
Bad mood can affect productivity.
(Unhappiness builds up over time.
Sudden depression may occur once every few days.
Reduces XP gain in all skills depending on
Unhappiness moodle, up to -10%.)
UI_trait_depressivemooddesc:
Often sad. May become depressed for no reason.
Bad mood can affect productivity.
(Unhappiness builds up over time.
Sudden depression may occur once every few days.
Reduces XP gain in all skills depending on
Unhappiness moodle, up to -10%.)

You have a dependence on alcohol. If you go too long without alcohol, you'll begin to feel stressed, then pain, and eventually withdrawal.
If you go through withdrawal, you will grow progressively more sick until you drink again or die.
You begin the game with one bottle of whiskey - better find more booze soon.
UI_trait_eagleeyeddesc:
Has a faster visibility fade and a higher visibility arc.

UI_trait_ectomorphdesc:
Trains Fitness faster, but trains Strength slower.
Prone to lose weight.
(+30% Fitness XP, -30% Strength XP,
+30% calorie burn if weight is over than 70.)
UI_trait_efficientdesc:
For some reason, you're faster than everyone else.
+3 Efficiency
UI_trait_emaciateddesc:
Low strength, low endurance and prone to injury.
(Lost if weight goes above 50.
-60% melee damage.
+20% chance to trip while run/sprint vaulting a low fence.
-10% chance to trip from getting attacked by lunging zombies.
Increased chance of falling when bumping into a zombie or survivor.
Greatly increased chance to fail a tall fence climb.
-70% endurance regeneration.
+40% fall damage, and increased chance of fracture/deep wound from falling.
Cannot gain Fitness XP.)
UI_trait_endomorphdesc:
Trains Strength faster, but trains Fitness slower.
Prone to gain weight.
(+30% Strength XP, -30% Fitness XP,
-30% calorie burn if weight is less than 90.)
UI_trait_enjoytheridedesc:
Driving at high speed reduces Stress and Unhappiness.

(Vehicle speed must be over 60 MPH.)

You're agile.

UI_trait_expertdriverdesc:
You drive cars much more effectively.
Vehicles move faster, make less noise, and handle more efficiently.
UI_trait_fastdesc:
You were always quick on your feet.

UI_trait_fastmetabolismdesc:
Prone to lose weight.
Trains Fitness faster, but Strength slower.
(+30% calorie burn if weight is more than 70,
+30% Fitness XP, -30% Strength XP.)

UI_trait_fearofthedarkdesc:
Fear of the dark. Panic without light sources.

Less knockback from melee weapons. Decreased carrying weight.
(Can be lost by training Stength to level 5.

UI_trait_fitdesc:
Fit.
(Can be gained by training Fitness to level 6.
Replaced by Athletic when reaching level 9 Fitness.
Lost if Fitness is below level 6.)

Has a chance of relapsing and becoming an Alcoholic again when drinking alcohol.

Has a chance of relapsing and becoming a Smoker again when using tobacco products.

Trains Strength and Fitness slower.

Transfers inventory items, refuels vehicles and drains gasoline faster.

UI_trait_genexpdesc:
Knows how to operate and repair generators.

You've always been the last in line.

You're particularly vulnerable and frail.

You become easily scratched from minor injuries.

You are adept at using crowbars as weapons.

UI_trait_gourmanddesc:
You delight in food of all kinds and find cooking an enjoyable sport.

Food that you cook is better.
Temperature of food and method of cooking does not bother you.
UI_trait_gracefuldesc:
Makes less noise when moving.
(-40% footsteps sound radius.
-10% chance to trip while run/sprint vaulting a low fence or getting attacked by lunging zombies.

While others find it difficult, you have no qualms pilfering the remains of the dead.

+1 Scavenging if Scavenging Skill mod enabled
UI_trait_gruntdesc: You worked some odd-jobs here and there.

+1 Aiming
+1 Reloading
+1 Nimble

UI_trait_gymgoerdesc:
You enjoy working out.
Exercise is twice as effective for you.

UI_trait_handydesc:
Faster and stronger constructions.
(+100 HP to all constructions except walls.
Increased building speed.
Slightly increased barricading speed.)
UI_trait_hardheardesc:
Smaller perception radius and hearing range.
(Some sound effects will be muffled.)
UI_trait_hardydesc:
Whether by force of will or physical fitness, you're not one to tire after long hours working.

UI_trait_heartyappetitedesc:
Needs to eat more regularly.
(+50% hunger.)
UI_trait_heavyaxemybeloveddesc:
Heavy axes like wood axes or battle axes consume less endurance when swinging.

UI_trait_highsweatydesc:
Increases wetness generated.

You like to keep your weight in check.

Your immune system is vulnerable to infection.

You have a 25% greater chance to become zombified

UI_trait_inattentivedesc: You cannot gain any xp in Scavenging, ever.

You're not as thorough as most.

You're one hard person to kill. Whenever you are reduced below 15% HP or dragged down, you are healed to full HP.

You have a knack for figuring things out.
You automatically know all recipes that would otherwise require a magazine or professional knowledge.

UI_trait_injureddesc:
You begin the game with moderate injuries.

UI_trait_keenhearingdesc:
Larger perception radius.
(+150% perception radius.

UI_trait_larkpersondesc:
Prefers to be awake in the morning.

UI_trait_leadfootdesc:
You've always had a heavy step and now it's time to put your foot down - on some zombies.

UI_trait_lesssweatydesc:
Reduces wetness generated.

UI_trait_lifelonglearnerdesc: Does not get bored while reading educational literature.
UI_trait_lighteaterdesc:
Needs to eat less regularly.
(-25% hunger)
UI_trait_liquidblooddesc:
Wounds bleeds more.
(+100% damage from bleeding.)
UI_trait_luckydesc:
Sometimes things just go your way.
+10% chance of finding rare loot.
-5% chance of failing item repairs.
UI_trait_marathonrunnerdesc:
Increases endurance recovery while running.
(Does not affect running while sneaking.)
UI_trait_martialdesc:
You've practiced martial arts and are versed at self-defense.
Your bare-handed attacks deal damage.
Damage scales with Short Blunt skill.
UI_trait_mesomorphdesc: Average body type with no pros or cons.
UI_trait_minersendurancedesc:
Has a small chance to partially recover endurance after a successful weapon attack.
Muscle strain recovers faster.
